event: 77 years old
male
exhibited a drowsy mental state
diagnosed with unstable angina
treated with a coronary stent insertion
treated with Sigmart (nicorandil 5 mg)
treated with aspirin (enteric coated tab, 100 mg)
treated with Almarl (arotinolol 5 mg)
treated with Herben (diltiazem 30 mg)
prior to surgery, chest X-ray was normal
echocardiogram showed a left ventricular ejection fraction of 61%
echocardiogram showed grade 2 diastolic dysfunction
echocardiogram showed mild aortic regurgitation
echocardiogram showed trivial mitral regurgitation and tricuspid regurgitation
all hematological parameters were unremarkable
aspirin was stopped 7 days before surgery
no medication was administered on the morning of surgery
initial vital signs were blood pressure (BP) of 144/79 mmHg
initial vital signs were heart rate (HR) of 68 beats/min
initial vital signs were SpO2 of 97%
bupivacaine (0.5%, 10 mg) was injected into the subarachnoid space at T10 for the spinal block
intravenous dexmedetomidine was infused
a pneumatic thigh tourniquet was applied intraoperatively at an inflation pressure of 300 mmHg for 100 min
total anesthesia time was 2 h and 10 min
during surgery, 600 mL of crystalloid was administered
during surgery, 150 mL of urine were collected
estimated blood loss was 90 mL
intraoperative vital signs showed BP of 100-130/50-70 mmHg
intraoperative vital signs showed HR of 45-55 beats/min
intraoperative vital signs showed SpO2 of 97%-99%
on arrival in the recovery room, initial vital signs were BP of 97/55 mmHg
on arrival in the recovery room, initial vital signs were HR of 41 beats/min
on arrival in the recovery room, initial vital signs were SpO2 of 100%
during the first hour of recovery, vital signs were maintained as follows: BP of 95-100/55-60 mmHg
during the first hour of recovery, vital signs were maintained as follows: HR of 40-45 beats/min
during the first hour of recovery, vital signs were maintained as follows: SpO2 of 98%-100%
about 90 mL of urine and 70 mL of blood were drained
400 mL of crystalloid was administered
postoperative pain was managed with an intravenous patient-controlled analgesia
complained of severe pain on the right thigh and overnight shivering
tridol injection (tramadol hydrochloride 50 mg/mL) was injected for pain control
physical examination showed the patient’s right thigh was stiff
physical examination showed the patient’s right thigh had turned a dark brown
physical examination showed the surgical site and distal extremity were unaffected
urine collected in a Foley bag was dark colored
the urine output every 8 h after operation was 100 mL, 220 mL, and 250 mL
immediately after surgery in the recovery room, laboratory results were within the normal range
about 19 h after surgery, CK was markedly increased to 2763 IU/L
about 19 h after surgery, creatine kinase-myocardial band (CK-MB) was elevated to 13.26 ng/mL
about 19 h after surgery, AST and BUN/Cr were also elevated
troponin-I was normal at 0.100 ng/mL
prolonged coagulation battery, thrombocytopenia, and high levels of fibrinogen degradation product and D-dimer were detected
serial tests demonstrated progressive deterioration of DIC
brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) was performed for differential diagnosis
mild small vessel disease was identified
serum myoglobin was 1027 ng/mL
random urine analysis revealed that urine myoglobin was 25.3 ng/mL
random urine analysis revealed that red blood cell count was 100/high powered field (HPF)
tourniquet-induced rhabdomyolysis was diagnosed
AKI (AKIN criteria stage 1) and DIC accompanied the rhabdomyolysis
BP dropped to 88/62 mmHg
norepinephrine (0.5-1 μg/kg/min) was infused
an arterial line was placed in the left radial artery for continuous monitoring of blood pressure
a central line was secured in the right internal jugular vein for adequate fluid supply and cardiovascular monitoring
a nephrologist was consulted
fluid treatment of 5 L/d was prescribed
dialysis was scheduled due to progressive renal impairment
fluid loading was initiated with normal saline at 400 mL/h for 2 h
for renoprotection, Lasix (furosemide, 20 mg injection) and sodium bicarbonate (40 mL injection of 8.4%) were administered
the urine output was 110 mL for the first 3 h and 600 mL for next 4 h
denogan (propacetamol hydrochloride 1 g injection) was administered to control fever
about 2 h after fluid administration, the patient’s mental status was restored to alert
the patient’s vital signs were as follows: BP of 90-110/60-70 mmHg
the patient’s vital signs were as follows: HR of 104 beats/min
the patient’s vital signs were as follows: body temperature of 36.2 °C
the patient’s vital signs were as follows: SpO2 of 98% on room air
after 2 h, the patient’s body temperature elevated again to 38.0 °C
approximately 5 h after the patient’s mental state was restored, his BP and HR dropped suddenly
cardiac arrest was performed for about 1 h
expired
* The number following 'diagnosed with unstable angina' is the number of hours before surgery.
